passages.passage_text: Directions (Q. 6-10): A word arrangement machine, when given an input of words, rearranges them following a particular rule in each step. The following is the illustration of the input and the steps of arrangement: Input: one cannot but feel sorry for him Step I: but cannot one sorry feel him for Step II: cannot but feel sorry one for him Step III: but cannot sorry feel him for one Step IV: sorry cannot but him feel one for And so on for subsequent steps. You have to find out the logic and answer the questions that follow.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If Step III reads "old streets of Calcutta attract me less", what will be the arrangement for Step VII?

choices.a: me of old attract less streets Calcutta
choices.b: less attract me of Calcutta streets old
choices.c: streets old Calcutta of me less attract
choices.d: Calcutta of streets old attract less me

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If Step IV reads "everyone were aware about their intimate things", what will be the middle three words of Step II?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If the given input is "he has learnt a lot from Krishna", what will be Step VI?

choices.a: he has from learnt lot Krishna
choices.b: has from he a Krishna lot learnt
choices.c: lot learnt Krishna a he has from
choices.d: Krishna a lot learnt from has he

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Input: he is member of the dancing club. For the above input, which step will be the following arrangement? Arrangement: he club the dancing member of is

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If Step V read "weeks of tepid slothful and weak performance", what would be Step VI?

choices.a: performance weeks and tepid of weak slothful
choices.b: of weeks and slothful tepid performance weak
choices.c: of tepid slothful performance weak and weeks
choices.d: performance and tepid weeks of weak slothful
